summary: The agent concludes that cavity detection via CBCT should only occur under specific circumstances where standard radiographs are insufficient; thus, reporting cavities in proximal surfaces during CBCT exams requires careful evaluation based on individual patient needs and diagnostic context. This nuanced view supports cautious inclusion—not universal practice—but acknowledges potential utility in selected cases involving difficult-to-detect lesions.
